questions.question: Describe the state of bits A and B after each ball is released (1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th ball) in the solved configuration.
questions.answer: 1st ball: A=left, B=left (initial state). 2nd ball: A=right, B=left (A flipped). 3rd ball: A=left, B=right (A flipped back, B flipped). 4th ball: A=right, B=right (both flipped).

questions.question: Where does the 4th ball go in the solved configuration, and why?
questions.answer: The 4th ball goes to the interceptor because both bits are pointing right, which routes the ball to the interceptor instead of the bottom output.

questions.question: This puzzle uses a 2-bit binary counter. What binary number is represented when both bits point right?
questions.answer: When both bits point right, the binary counter represents the number 3 (in binary: 11). This is the 4th state since counting starts from 0.
